notes: BLOCKER: no small-VLM GGUF (<=3B) present on rig. /opt/ai-models/ inventory confirms only Qwen2.5-VL-7B (frozen, 7B, already failed at 600 s/page) plus non-VLM text models. One orphan mmproj-gemma4-e4b-f16.gguf (945 MB) exists but its base gemma-4-E4B-it-Q4_K_M.gguf is a ~4B model, still exceeds 3B budget and Gemma-3/4-V was flagged in memory as poor Devanagari. Per B7 step 2, halting before any multi-GB download. User approval required.
rig_inventory_ai_models: gemma-4-E4B-it-Q4_K_M.gguf (4.7G, text or paired with mmproj below)

acquisition_plan.model: MiniCPM-V 2.6 (8B — slightly over 3B budget but best Vulkan-track record for OCR-per-VRAM)
acquisition_plan.caveat: This is 8B, not <=3B. Only worth pursuing if user relaxes size budget; not inside current pass criterion.

acquisition_plan.model: Qwen2-VL-2B-Instruct (<=3B, direct small sibling of frozen 7B)
acquisition_plan.caveat: Same vision-tower family as the 7B that failed — smaller CLIP may help, but architecture risk: if Vulkan bottleneck was the encoder design itself (not parameter count), this inherits it. Cheapest to test.
Smallest and newest. Devanagari coverage unknown — SmolVLM training corpus is English-heavy. Accept Devanagari-fidelity risk.

recommendation: Ask user to approve one download. Default pick: rank 2 (Qwen2-VL-2B, 2.2 GB) because it most directly tests the hypothesis that the CLIP tower shrinks with the model. If user wants minimum disk and maximum novelty, pick rank 3 (SmolVLM, 1.5 GB) but Devanagari is a coin flip.
next_actions_if_all_fail: Revert to Option D (park OCR for v2 ship). 471 image PDFs remain queued.
